---
title: "Bringing Work Trips Closer to Home"
subtitle: "WF-TDM Sketch-Level HBW Trip Redistribution Test"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    transition: fade
    embed-resources: true
    width: 1280
    height: 720
    fig-align: center
    footer: "Wasatch Front Regional Council | Bringing Work Trips Closer to Home"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
jupyter: july2025
---

```{python}
#| label: setup

import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px
import os
import sys

sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join('..', '..', '..', 'run_sets', 'bring-work-trips-closer-to-home'))
import report_loader as loader

frames = loader.load()
scenario_ids = frames['scenario_ids']
vmt_vhd = frames['vmt_vhd_by_county_facility']
vht_hh = frames['vht_per_household']
transit = frames['transit_ridership']
trip_length = frames['hbw_trip_length']
trip_length_dist = frames['hbw_trip_length_distribution']
corridor_volumes = frames['corridor_volumes']

GEO_ORDER = loader.GEO_ORDER
GEO_COLORS = {'City Area': '#2a78d6', 'Medium District': '#1baf7a', 'Workshop Area': '#eda100'}
COUNTY_ORDER = ['Salt Lake', 'Davis', 'Utah', 'Weber', 'Box Elder', 'Region']
shifted_ids = [s for s in scenario_ids if s != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO]
```

## The Question

::: {.incremental}
- What if more people who live in an area also **worked** in that area — instead of commuting elsewhere?
- Sketch test: shift 5% / 10% / 25% of home-based-work trips leaving a target area back into it, following today's existing destination pattern
- Tested at three geography scales: **City Area** (lead), Medium District, Workshop Area — each region-wide
- 2027 RTP adoption-year network, model v10.0.0-beta.1
:::

::: {.notes}
This is a directional, order-of-magnitude check, not a land-use forecast — see the caveats slide.
:::

## Data Completeness {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
from IPython.display import Markdown, display
display(Markdown(
    f"**{len(shifted_ids)} of 9** planned shift scenarios verified so far: "
    f"{', '.join(shifted_ids)} — all three geography types at the **10% shift level**. "
    "5% and 25% not yet run."
))
```

::: {.callout-note}
This deck extends automatically as more scenarios complete.
:::

## Congestion: Region-Wide VHD {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
vhd_region = vmt_vhd[(vmt_vhd['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vmt_vhd['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO)]
vhd_region = vhd_region.groupby('geography_label', as_index=False)['delta_DY_VHD'].sum()

fig = px.bar(
    vhd_region, x='geography_label', y='delta_DY_VHD', color='geography_label',
    category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
    labels={'geography_label': '', 'delta_DY_VHD': 'Δ Daily VHD (hrs)'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Daily Vehicle-Hours of Delay, Region<br><sup>10% shift level</sup>', font_size=15))
fig.show()
```

## VHD by County {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
vhd_df = vmt_vhd.copy()
vhd_df['CO_NAME'] = pd.Categorical(vhd_df['CO_NAME'], categories=COUNTY_ORDER, ordered=True)
vhd_by_county = vhd_df[vhd_df['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].groupby(
    ['CO_NAME', 'geography_label'], as_index=False, observed=True
)['delta_DY_VHD'].sum()

fig = px.bar(
    vhd_by_county, x='CO_NAME', y='delta_DY_VHD', color='geography_label', barmode='group',
    category_orders={'CO_NAME': COUNTY_ORDER, 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
    labels={'CO_NAME': 'County', 'delta_DY_VHD': 'Δ Daily VHD (hrs)', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Daily VHD by County', font_size=15))
fig.show()
```

## Why: Vehicle Miles Traveled {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
vmt_by_county = vhd_df[vhd_df['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO].groupby(
    ['CO_NAME', 'geography_label'], as_index=False, observed=True
)['delta_DY_VMT'].sum()

fig = px.bar(
    vmt_by_county, x='CO_NAME', y='delta_DY_VMT', color='geography_label', barmode='group',
    category_orders={'CO_NAME': COUNTY_ORDER, 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
    labels={'CO_NAME': 'County', 'delta_DY_VMT': 'Δ Daily VMT (mi)', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Daily VMT by County', font_size=15))
fig.show()
```

::: {.notes}
Shorter commutes mean less driving; less driving on congested facilities is why delay falls.
:::

## Corridor Highlights — City Area {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
city_corr = corridor_volumes[corridor_volumes['geography_label'] == 'City Area'].sort_values('delta_DY_VHD').head(8)

fig = px.bar(
    city_corr, x='corridor_label', y='delta_DY_VHD', color_discrete_sequence=[GEO_COLORS['City Area']],
    labels={'corridor_label': 'Corridor', 'delta_DY_VHD': 'Δ Daily VHD (hrs)'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(height=460, xaxis_tickangle=-30, title=dict(text='Top 8 Corridors by VHD Reduction — City Area, 10%', font_size=15))
fig.show()
```

I-15, Redwood Road, Mountain View Corridor, and I-215 see the largest reductions.

## Transit Ridership {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
fig = px.bar(
    transit[transit['scenario_id'] != loader.BASELINE_SCENARIO],
    x='line_label', y='delta_Boardings', color='geography_label', barmode='group',
    category_orders={'line_label': ['FrontRunner', 'TRAX Blue Line', 'TRAX Green Line', 'TRAX Red Line'], 'geography_label': GEO_ORDER},
    color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
    labels={'line_label': 'Line', 'delta_Boardings': 'Δ Daily Boardings', 'geography_label': 'Geography'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='Change in Daily Boardings — FrontRunner &amp; TRAX', font_size=15))
fig.show()
```

Shorter commutes mean fewer trips long enough for transit to compete on — ridership dips slightly on every line.

## Free Time &amp; Trip Length {.smaller}

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
region_hh = vht_hh[(vht_hh['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vht_hh['geography_label'].isin(GEO_ORDER))]
fig = px.bar(
    region_hh, x='geography_label', y='delta_VHT_PER_HH', color='geography_label',
    category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
    labels={'geography_label': '', 'delta_VHT_PER_HH': 'Δ Peak VHT/HH (hrs)'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=420, title=dict(text='Peak VHT / Household', font_size=14))
fig.show()
```
:::
::: {.column width="50%"}
```{python}
region_tl = trip_length[(trip_length['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (trip_length['geography_label'].isin(GEO_ORDER))]
fig = px.bar(
    region_tl, x='geography_label', y='delta_trip_length', color='geography_label',
    category_orders={'geography_label': GEO_ORDER}, color_discrete_map=GEO_COLORS,
    labels={'geography_label': '', 'delta_trip_length': 'Δ HBW Trip Length (mi)'},
)
fig.add_hline(y=0, line_color='#c3c2b7', line_width=1)
fig.update_layout(showlegend=False, height=420, title=dict(text='Avg. HBW Trip Length', font_size=14))
fig.show()
```
:::
::::

## Trip Length Distribution {.smaller}

```{python}
#| fig-align: center
td = trip_length_dist.copy()
fig = px.line(
    td, x='bin_label', y='share_pct', color='geography_label', markers=True,
    category_orders={'bin_label': loader.DISTANCE_BIN_LABELS, 'geography_label': ['Baseline'] + GEO_ORDER},
    color_discrete_map={**GEO_COLORS, 'Baseline': '#898781'},
    labels={'bin_label': 'HBW Trip Length', 'share_pct': 'Share of Trips (%)', 'geography_label': 'Scenario'},
)
fig.update_layout(height=460, title=dict(text='HBW Trip Length Distribution, Baseline vs. 10% Shift', font_size=15))
fig.show()
```

Shorter trips become more common, longer trips less common — the mechanism working as intended, region-wide.

## Cross-Scale Comparison {.smaller}

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
rows = []
for geo in GEO_ORDER:
    vhd = vmt_vhd[(vmt_vhd['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vmt_vhd['geography_label'] == geo)]['delta_DY_VHD'].sum()
    vmt = vmt_vhd[(vmt_vhd['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vmt_vhd['geography_label'] == geo)]['delta_DY_VMT'].sum()
    hh = vht_hh[(vht_hh['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (vht_hh['geography_label'] == geo)]['delta_VHT_PER_HH'].iloc[0]
    tl = trip_length[(trip_length['CO_NAME'] == 'Region') & (trip_length['geography_label'] == geo)]['delta_trip_length'].iloc[0]
    rows.append({'Geography': geo, 'Δ VHD (hrs)': f'{vhd:+,.0f}', 'Δ VMT (mi)': f'{vmt:+,.0f}',
                 'Δ Peak VHT/HH': f'{hh:+.3f}', 'Δ Trip Length (mi)': f'{tl:+.2f}'})
display(pd.DataFrame(rows).style.hide(axis='index')
        .set_table_styles([{'selector': 'th', 'props': [('background-color', '#2a78d6'), ('color', 'white')]}]))
```

City Area and Medium District track closely; Workshop Area is consistently smaller — the coarsest geography, fewer areas region-wide.

## Caveats {.smaller}

:::  {.incremental}
- **Not a real land-use/economic forecast** — no businesses relocate, no zoning changes simulated
- **Feasibility not checked** — model doesn't confirm the area could hold the added jobs
- **Sketch-level, order-of-magnitude** — trips manually reallocated on today's existing pattern
- **HBW trips only** — other trip purposes untouched
- **No second-order effects** — housing costs, relocation, business response not captured
- **Same mechanism at all shift levels** — 5/10/25% test sensitivity to size, not to method
:::

## Questions

**[Full write-up →](summary.html)**
